title: Long-run aggregate supply
description: Practice what you've learned about why long-run aggregate supply curve (LRAS) is vertical, and the relationship between LRAS and the stock of resources, technology, and the natural rate of unemployment in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how wages respond to changes in the price level in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wages fall when the price level falls and rise when the price level rises
Wages fall when the price level rises and rise when the price level falls
Wages do not change when the price level falls and rise when the price level rises
Wages do not change when the price level increases and wages fall when the price level decreases
Wages do not adjust to changes in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements makes a correct distinction between the short run and the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Short run: wages are fixed; long run: wages are fully flexible
Short run: aggregate supply slopes downwards; long run: aggregate supply slopes upwards
Short run: wages are fully flexible; long run: wages are fixed
Short run: less than six months has passed; long run: more than six months has passed
Short run: unemployment will increase in response to inflation; long run: unemployment is not affected by inflation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the relationship between inflation and unemployment in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Unemployment is not affected by inflation in the long run
Inflation and unemployment are positively related
Inflation and unemployment are negatively related
The rate of inflation is equal to the unemployment rate
The unemployment rate and the inflation rate are both equal to zero in the long run

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement correctly describes the difference between short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) and long-run aggregate supply (LRAS)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a tradeoff between inflation and unemployment in the SRAS curve but not with the LRAS curve.
Prices and wages are flexible in the SRAS curve, but the LRAS curve assumes that prices and wages are fixed.
Any period less than six months is represented with the SRAS curve, but LRAS refers to any period beyond that.
The SRAS curve is upward sloping, but LRAS is downward sloping.
SRAS is downward sloping, but LRAS is upward sloping.

assessment_items.question_content:
*The country of Verminville produces only two goods: cat food and mouse traps. The production possibilities curve (PPC) here represents five possible levels of output in Verminville.*

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the levels of output in the PPC shown here corresponds to Verminville's maximum sustainable output and its long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots one concave production possibilities curve and five labeled points. The horizontal axis is labeled Cat food, and the vertical axis is labeled Mouse traps. The curve slopes downward from left to right. Point A lies on the curve. Points B and C lie inside the curve. Points D and E lie outside the curve.

choices.content: A only
B and C
E and D
A and C
A, E, and D

assessment_items.question_content:
The PPC for Verminville is shown here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which points are associated with the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve for Verminville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots one concave production possibilities curve and four labeled points. The horizontal axis is labeled Cat food, and the vertical axis is labeled Mouse traps. The curve slopes downward from left to right. Points A and B lie on the curve. Point C lies inside the curve. Point D lies outside the curve.

choices.content: A or B
C or D
A only
A and C
D and B

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

The LRAS curve for Verminville is shown here. 

**Which points correspond to a point on Verminville's production possibilities curve (PPC)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots one vertical line labeled L R A S and three labeled points: S, T, and U. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. Point T is located at the base of the vertical L R A S line. Point S is to the left of the vertical line, and point U is to the right. All three points lie on the horizontal axis.

choices.content: Point T only
Points S, T, and U
Point S only
Point U only
Points S and U only

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ radio 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve and the production possibilities curve (PPC) for the nation of Hamiltonia are shown here:


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]



**Which point or points have a value of \$6 billion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane displays one vertical line labeled L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are marked with arrowheads and extend outward. The vertical L R A S line intersects the horizontal axis at the value 6 billion dollars, which is labeled directly below the line.

A coordinate plane plots a curved line representing a production possibilities curve. The horizontal axis is labeled Consumption goods, and the vertical axis is labeled Capital. The axes are unmarked. Five points are plotted and labeled: Point X lies on the curve in the upper middle region, Point R lies on the curve toward the lower right, Point S is above and to the left of the curve, Point Y is below the curve, and Point Z is above and to the right of the curve.

choices.content: X and R
R only
X only
X, R, Y, and S
Z only

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes why the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve is upward sloping but the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve is vertical?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Some expectations are incorrect in the short run, but all expectations are correct in the long run.
Wages and prices are fully flexible in the short run and the long run.
Wages and prices are flexible in the short run but not the long run.
Wages and prices take at least six months to adjust.
There is a tradeoff between inflation and unemployment in the long run but not the short run.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why long-run aggregate supply is vertical at full employment output, as opposed to some other output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Full employment output is the output associated with the natural rate of unemployment
Full employment output means a country has an unemployment rate higher than the natural rate
Full employment output means a country has an unemployment rate lower than the natural rate
Output more than full employment output is impossible
Output will always return to the level at which unemployment is zero percent

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains why the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve is vertical?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nominal wages eventually adjust to changes in the price level
Nominal wages are slow to adjust to changes in the price level
Full employment output depends on the price level
The spending multiplier is equal to zero
Households save a large share of additional income
